candidate_problems.text: How can 'constraint propagation' be operationalized so that an evaluator can distinguish principled reconstruction from ad hoc repair after perturbation?
Are there important explanations with low apparent coupling at the verbal level but high underlying load-bearing structure, and how would the proposed test avoid misclassifying them?
What representation of objects, relations, assumptions, and invariants is sufficient for perturbation tests to track explanatory stiffness rather than surface wording dependence?
